# Figure description: Картограмма_магниторазведки.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Картограмма_магниторазведки.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index/coverage map** (карта фактического материала / обзорная карта) for **magnetic survey data (магниторазведка)**. Specifically, it is a spatial index map showing the distribution and scales of magnetic survey coverage blocks/polygons across the study area, with survey profile lines overlaid. It is Drawing No. 23, Sheet 6 (Чертёж №23, Лист 6), dated 1984.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large portion of the northern Kazakhstan survey area associated with the **Западно-Тенизская партия ЦГФП (West-Teniz Party of the Central Geophysical Field Expedition)**. The figure indexes magnetic survey coverage for the period **1978–1984**. Visible map sheet codes and sector labels include:

- **Roman numeral block designations**: I, IV, V, VII, VIII, IX, X, XV, and others
- **Profile/traverse line labels** (грp prefix): ГРIX-0, ГРIX-720, ГРVIII-0, ГРVIII-800, ГРVII-370, ГРVI-668, ГРVI-650, ГРIV-650, ГРIII-800, ГРIII-963, ГРII-490, ГРИI-324, ГРX-0, ГРX-202, ГРXI-0, ГРXII-490, ГРXIII-0, ГРXIV-694, ГРXIV-294 (approximately), ГРXV-0, and others
- **Sheet/block labels**: X-XXIII, XXXIV, XXXV, and others in the upper portion
- **Numerical contour/anomaly values** annotating individual polygons: e.g., 93, 53, 463, 335, 300, 378, 338, 610, 318, 279, 85, 35, 36, 368, 322, 371, 431, 402, 461, 291, 589, 231, 314, 311, 169, 225, 250, 365, 162, 302, 228, 502, 167, 189, 190, 196, 181, 161, 258, 248, 102, 189, 324, 325, 595, and many others

---

## Key Content

The map displays a **patchwork of rectangular and irregular polygonal blocks**, each representing an area covered by magnetic survey at a particular scale. The blocks are differentiated by **line style/hatching** according to survey scale:

- **Solid diagonal hatching** (dense): Scale 1:500,000
- **Medium diagonal lines** (–II–): Scale 1:200,000
- **Light/dashed diagonal lines**: Scale 1:100,000
- **Solid bold diagonal lines**: Scale 1:50,000

The **red/dark-red lines** overlaid on the map represent **ЦГФП survey profiles from 1980** (Профили ЦГФП за 1980 г.), shown as irregular traverse lines cutting diagonally across the block grid — these are the actual geophysical measurement traverses. They run predominantly in a northwest-southeast to north-south orientation and concentrate in the central and southwestern portions of the map.

**Numbered polygons** (e.g., "–371–" in a box) refer to **catalogue contour/anomaly numbers** (номер контура по каталогу), allowing cross-reference to an accompanying magnetic anomaly catalogue.

The overall pattern shows:
- **Denser, higher-resolution coverage** (1:50,000 and 1:100,000) in the central and lower portions of the map
- **Regional-scale coverage** (1:200,000–1:500,000) in the upper/northeastern blocks
- A **stamp of the Казахская ССР, Региональный геологический фонд** (Kazakh SSR Regional Geological Fund) with accession number **36572**, confirming this belongs to Report 36572

---

## Text and Labels

**Title:** МАГНИТОРАЗВЕДКА

**Drawing reference:** Чертёж №23, Лист 6

**Date:** 1984 г.

**Attribution line (bottom right):** К отчёту Западно-Тенизской партии ЦГФП за 1978–84 г.г.

**Legend heading:** УСЛОВНЫЕ ОБОЗНАЧЕНИЯ:

**Legend entries:**
- Масштаб 1:500 000 (dense hatching symbol)
- –II– 1:200 000 (medium line symbol)
- –II– 1:100 000 (dashed symbol)
- Масштаб 1:50 000 (bold diagonal symbol)
- Профили ЦГФП за 1980 г. (red line with box label "ГРЖ ПО")
- Номер контура по каталогу (boxed number, e.g. –371–)

**Archive stamp:** Казахская ССР, Региональный геологический фонд, Инв. № 36572

**Profile line labels (selected):** ГРIX-0, ГРIX-720, ГРVIII-0, ГРVIII-800, ГРVII-370, ГРVI-668, ГРIV-650, ГРIII-800, ГРIII-963, ГРV-0, ГРV-963 (approx.), ГРXIV-694, ГРXII-490, ГРX-0, ГРX-202, ГРI-0 (approx.)

**Block/sheet labels:** I, IV, V, VII, VIII, IX, X, XV, X-XXIII, XXXIV, XXXV

---

## Research Value

This index map tells a geologist **where magnetic survey data exist, at what resolution, and how the survey traverses were laid out** across the study area — essential for assessing data coverage gaps and the reliability of any magnetic anomaly interpretations. The catalogue numbers on each polygon allow direct cross-referencing to the magnetic anomaly database, enabling identification of specific anomalies relevant to mineral targeting (e.g., iron ore, mafic/ultramafic bodies, or structural lineaments detectable by magnetics
